input.user_input: Insert a mutual confidentiality clause after Section 5 of the consulting agreement. It should cover both parties and last 3 years after termination.

actual:
It looks like you haven't uploaded or attached the consulting agreement yet. 

If you upload the document, I can insert the clause directly into the text, renumber the subsequent sections, and update any cross-references for you. 

In the meantime, here is a standard mutual confidentiality clause drafted to your specifications that you can drop in as the new Section 6:

**6. Confidentiality**

**6.1 Definition of Confidential Information.** "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other party (the "Receiving Party"), whether disclosed orally, in writing, or by inspection of tangible objects, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. 

**6.2 Mutual Obligations.** The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of like kind, but in no event less than reasonable care; (c) not disclose such Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, except to its personnel who have a need to know and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein; and (d) not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to perform its obligations or exercise its rights under this Agreement.

**6.3 Exclusions.** Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes generally known to the public through no fault or breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession at the time of disclosure without restriction on use or disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on disclosure.

**6.4 Survival.** The obligations set forth in this Section 6 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall remain in full force and effect for a period of three (3) years following such expiration or termination.

***
